diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Persistent craniopharyngeal canal with sphenoid meningoencephalocele
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on MR imaging showing a large meningoencephalocoele in the left nasopharynx, with the neck of the sac traced into the subarachnoid space through a canal in the sphenoid bone. CT imaging confirmed the cranial end of the defect starting at the tuberculum sellae and the chiasmatic sulcus, passing through the non-pneumatised body sphenoid, opening inferiorly through the ala of the vomer on the left side. The patient's history of cleft palate and the presence of neuroglia within the canal supported the diagnosis.
